title: Xyridion pseudacorus
taxonomy: Xyridion pseudacorus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Xyridaceae. The Xyridaceae family is a group of plants commonly known as the yellow-eyed grasses or pygmy cypress. This family is part of the order Poales, which includes various monocotyledonous plants such as grasses and sedges. Xyridion pseudacorus is native to South America and is often found in wetland environments. The classification of Xyridion pseudacorus is further supported by its unique characteristics and genetic analysis.

parts: Xyridion pseudacorus, also known as Yellow Flag, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The rhizomes of the Yellow Flag are considered to be the medicinal parts of the plant. They contain bioactive compounds such as iridoids and flavonoids, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The rhizomes are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Xyridion pseudacorus for medicinal purposes.
